name: New Orleans Red Beans and Rice
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon fresh thyme, or dried thyme if fresh is not available
2 Smoked Turkey Legs, cut into pieces by a butcher
Prudholm's Poultry or Meat or Soup Magic (it really doesn't matter)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and soak the beans overnight.
2. In the morning, rinse and discard the soaking water.
3. Place the onion, celery, garlic, turkey legs, bay leaves, and thyme on the bottom of the Crock Pot.
4. Pour beans on top.
5. Add water to cover the beans in the Crock Pot.
6. Stir in hot sauce (You may need to maneuver the ingredients to get the top covered; however, leave the lid closed for the first few hours so the beans can cook down).
7. Cook on high for 1 hour and reduce to low for 7-8 hours (sometimes you may want to go a little longer if they are not tender; going up to 10 hours will not hurt).
8. When beans are done, remove approximately 1 cup and mash to a paste.
9. Add the mashed paste back to the pot and stir.
10. Turn off the Crock Pot and let stand for about 1 hour.
11. Serve over Uncle Ben's rice with smoked sausage and hot sauce.
Note: They may be a little thick, so adjust the water and amount of beans to your liking. This makes enough for at least 3-4 meals for a family of 4 (and we have a teenage son!).
